subject: Mathematics
title: Penrose Tiles
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Penrose Tiles.

hook.text: Roger Penrose discovered two shapes that tile a plane forever without ever repeating.

buildup.text: His 'kite' and 'dart' tiles fit together following simple matching rules based on the golden ratio.

discovery.text: The resulting pattern covers the plane completely but never develops a repeating unit cell.

twist.text: In 2023, a hobbyist found a single shape that tiles aperiodically—the 'einstein' tile.

climax.text: Aperiodic tilings explain quasicrystals and challenge the assumption that order requires repetition.

punchline.text: Two shapes, no repeats, infinite coverage. Math at its finest.

quiz.question: What is special about Penrose tiles?
options: They tile the plane without ever repeating
They only work on curved surfaces
